data.title: Is it time to become bullish again? Here is what Sandeep Tandon is betting on
data.description:
Sandeep Tandon, Founder and CIO, Quant Mutual Fund

Sandeep Tandon-led Quant Mutual Fund believes the risk-off phase in the Indian equity markets is nearing its conclusion. He said that while the market sentiment has become excessively negative, with several segments now falling into the 'neglected or hated' category, it's the right time to adopt a more constructive stance.

"From our perspective at Quant Mutual Fund, the timing is right to become constructive on the markets. We believe that the market has potential to recover and gradually rally from this point on," Tandon said.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

He also said that while both economic and corporate data have shown signs of deterioration, these underwhelming indicators are largely priced into the markets.

Follow our live blog for all the market action

In its monthly note to investors, Quant Mutual Fund said, "Although high level macro-economic and corporate earnings data merit tracking, most, if not all of these below-estimates data are factored in and timing is ripe for being optimistic about selective segments of the market. Hence, we are now close to fully deployed and believe that risk-off phase is in its fag-end stage."

October saw Foreign Institutional Investors (FIIs) selling Indian equities worth Rs 1.1 lakh crore. Factors such as weaker-than-expected earnings, China's stimulus measures, and optimism around the U.S. economy with Donald Trump's return to power drove this trend. The sell-off pushed the Nifty 50 down by 8 percent from its all-time high of 26,277.35 and the Sensex nearly 7 percent below its peak of 85,978.25.

Also Read | Quant Mutual Fund ramps up leadership with seven top hires, appoints new CEO

However, with the 'Sell India, Buy China' strategy fading and the Trump trade nearing its final stretch, FIIs turned net buyers last week, infusing $0.5 billion into Indian equities after eight consecutive weeks of selling.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Quant Mutual Fund, founded by Sandeep Tandon, got a mutual fund license from the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) in 2017. It has been the fastest growing mutual fund in the country, with assets growing from Rs 100-odd crore in 2019 to more than Rs 1 lah crore currently.

data.title: C2C Advanced Systems shares list at 90% premium over IPO price on NSE
data.description:
C2C Advanced Systems shares listed on the NSE Emerge platform on December 3.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

C2C Advanced Systems shares listed on the NSE SME Emerge platform with a premium of 90 percent on December 3. The share listing took place following the postponement of the company's earlier listing date due to regulatory concerns. The shares were allotted to the successful bidders a day earlier on Monday.

The shares of C2C Advanced Systems were listed at Rs 429.40 per share on the NSE, a premium of 90 percent against the issue price of Rs 226.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Ahead of the listing, the shares of C2C Advanced Systems were commanding a grey market premium of Rs 240 over the IPO price.

The Rs 99-crore C2C Advanced Systems initial public offering (IPO) faced a setback, with over 3.72 lakh applications worth Rs 27 crore being withdrawn over SEBI action.

C2C Advanced Systems IPO saw an overwhelming initial interest in is maiden share sale. The shares were available for subscription in the price band of Rs 214-226 apiece.

C2C Advanced Systems finalises IPO allotment amid SEBI's action; GMP rises to nearly 90%

The public issue of C2C Advanced Systems, which specialises in defence and aerospace solutions, had seen demand soaring to 125 times with investors bidding for 36.56 crore shares, while only 29.15 lakh shares were available.

data.title: Sensex sustains above 80k as markets rise for third straight session; metals, PSU rally, FMCG stocks lag
data.description:
Adani Ports, Shriram Finance, SBI, HDFC Bank, and Adani Enterprises were the top gainers on the Nifty.

Benchmark indices Nifty and Sensex opened the session on a decent note, albeit marginally higher and extending gains for a third trading session in a row after metal and PSU bank stocks helped bolster sentiment in the market. US markets also helped lift sentiment where Nasdaq and S&P 500 posted record closing highs led by a sharp rally in technology stocks.

This week, markets will also be taking cues from RBI's MPC meeting scheduled for December 4. A Moneycontrol poll of analysts and fund managers suggests that the central bank will likely keep interest rates unchanged.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

At about 9:40 am, the Sensex was up 100.10 points or 0.12 percent at 80,348.18, and the Nifty was up 33.00 points or 0.14 percent at 24,309.05. About 2,315 shares advanced, 680 shares declined, and 106 shares unchanged.

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

"The market's undertone remains positive, as there are no immediate negative triggers and weak GDP figures were already priced in," said Ajit Mishra, Senior Vice President at Religare Broking, in a conversation with Moneycontrol. He noted that investors are now eyeing government support through capital expenditure, which could further bolster sentiment. The central bank could cut CRR which could boost liquidity While the pace of FII selling may ease by mid-December, Mishra expects it to continue until early 2025.

To be sure, foreign institutional investors (FIIs) sold Rs 239 crore to kickstart December while domestic institutional investors mopped up Rs 3,588 crore.

Also read: ITC, Godfrey Phillips shares in focus as GST panel suggests 35% tax for cigarettes, tobacco

Tobacco major ITC tanked 3 percent after the Group of Ministers on GST rate rationalisation recommended raising tax on aerated beverages, cigarettes, tobacco and related 'sin' products to 35 percent from the current 28 percent, CNBC-Awaaz along with PTI News said quoting sources familiar with the development. Pepsi bottler Varun Beverages opened 5 percent lower in today's session.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Newly-listed food delivery major Swiggy rallied 4 percent ahead of its Q2 earnings, the first since its debut last month. Prosus, one of the company’s largest shareholders, said in its half-yearly disclosures that Swiggy has reported a revenue of $750 million (around Rs 6,300 crore) in the first half of the financial year 2025 (H1FY25), up 40 percent from $536 million (about Rs 4,500 crore) recorded during the same period last year.

Read more: Godrej Properties QIP saw four-fold demand, Pirojsha says funds to help in growth

KPI Green Energy shares rallied over 3 percent after receiving its largest order worth Rs 1,311 crore from Coal India. The company is going to set up a 300 MWAC (405 MWDC) ground-mounted Solar PV plant including Operation & Maintenance (O&M) services on a comprehensive basis for five years on an engineering, procurement, and construction basis in Gujarat.

The mid-small cap indices outperformed the frontline indices with gains of 0.6 and 0.5 percent, respectively. Market experts suggest that BSE Small-Cap and Mid-Cap have seen a decent correction in the past two months and could likely see renewed buying interest in the coming weeks. Smallcap stocks saw a strong rally on Tuesday, with 750 BSE Smallcap stocks trading in the positive, while 173 stocks declined. Midcap stocks also witnessed a similar trend as 100 shares rose, while just 29 traded in the negative.

Nifty Bank, PSU Bank, Realty and Metal Index rallied the most, rising 1 percent each. Gains in major banking names such as SBI, HDFC Bank, IndusInd Bank, and Shriram Finance lifted the index. The Realty index, up over 3 percent in a week, traded higher led by majors like Godrej Properties, Prestige and Oberoi Realty.

"Immediate support is placed at 24,000 and 23,900, historically strong reversal zones that may present buying opportunities if confirmed by favourable price action. On the upside, 24,350 serves as the first hurdle. A decisive breakout above this level could drive the index toward the next resistance targets at 24,800 and 25,000, unlocking significant upside potential," Mandar Bhojane of Choice Broking said. "Traders are advised to adopt a cautious stance and wait for confirmation of price action at key support and resistance levels before initiating fresh positions," he added.

Adani Ports, Shriram Finance, SBI, HDFC Bank, and Adani Enterprises were the top gainers on the Nifty. ITC, Bharti Airtel, Sun Pharma, Trent and M&M were the key laggards.

data.title: Varun Beverages shares fall 5% on possible hike in GST rate to 35%
data.description:
Varun Beverages shares have surged 45 percent in a year.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of PepsiCo bottler Varun Beverages cracked 5% in the morning deals on December 3, a day after report suggested the GST rate on aerated beverages might be hiked to 35%, up from the current 28%.

At 9.40 am, Varun Beverages shares were quoting Rs 621.45, lower by 1.7% on the NSE after trimming some of its early losses.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Follow our market blog to catch more updates

The Group of Ministers on GST rate rationalisation has recommended to raise tax on aerated beverages, cigarettes, tobacco and related 'sin' products to 35% from the current 28%, CNBC-Awaaz along with PTI News said quoting sources familiar with the development on December 2.

The proposal implies a new 35% GST rate slab could be created for tobacco and related products, as recommended by the GoM.

"The GoM has agreed to propose a special rate of 35% on tobacco and related products and aerated beverages. The four-tier tax slab of 5, 12, 18, and 28% will continue and a new rate of 35% is proposed by the GoM," an official told PTI News.

India's carbonated soft drinks segment struggles to achieve its scale potential due to hurdles like high GST taxation, despite government initiatives such as 'Make in India' and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat,' according to a report by economic think tank ICRIER.

"However, despite government initiatives like 'Make in India' and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat', the carbonated soft drinks segment is unable to reach its potential in terms of scale expansion due to barriers such as the high tax brackets and compensation cess under the GST regime, implemented since 2017," said ICRIER.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Varun Beverages shares have surged 45 percent in a year, as compared to the Nifty 50's run of 18 percent during the same time period.

data.title: Indegene block deal: 4.7% stake sold for Rs 707 crore, CA Dawn likely offloads part equity post IPO
data.description:
Indegene’s equity structure is dominated by public shareholders, who collectively hold over 92 percent of the company.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Indegene shares saw a 4.7 percent equity stake sale in block deals aggregating as much as Rs 707 crore on Tuesday, 3 December, with private equity firm CA Dawn likely offloading a part of its shareholding. The deal involved the sale of 1.13 crore shares at an average price of Rs 623 per share, reported CNBC TV18.

Prior to the block deal, CA Dawn held a 14.52 percent stake in Indegene as of September 2024. This sale could potentially cut its holding to below 10 percent. CA Dawn, a significant investor in Indegene, had already trimmed its stake during the company’s Rs 1,840 crore initial public offering in May 2024. At the time of the IPO, CA Dawn (Carlyle-affiliated) owned a 20.42 percent stake in the company, which decreased after it sold shares worth Rs 1,082 crore in the offer for sale.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Indegene’s equity structure is dominated by public shareholders, who collectively hold over 92 percent of the company. Foreign institutional investors (FIIs) and domestic institutional investors (DIIs) own 4.82 percent and 3.17 percent stakes, respectively.

Also read | Quant Mutual Fund ramps up leadership with seven top hires, appoints new CEO

Indegne, known for providing digital-led commercialisation services to the life sciences sector, had a successful IPO earlier this year, which was oversubscribed significantly.

Details regarding the buyers in the latest transaction were not immediately available.

Indegene share price fell on Tuesday, in the aftermath of the block deal. The stock was trading down 7.5 percent at Rs 618 on NSE. The company’s current market capitalisation is at about Rs 14,800 crore. Indegene stock has jumped over 38 percent from its IPO allotment price of Rs 452.

data.title: Quant Mutual Fund ramps up leadership with seven top hires, appoints new CEO
data.description:
As we move forward, we will invest further in expanding our teams and capabilities, wrote the fund house.


Sandeep Tandon-owned Quant Mutual Fund has significantly expanded its senior management, onboarding seven new leaders, including a CEO, in the past nine months. This move signals a shift from its owner-driven, low-cost model towards a more institutionalized framework akin to industry norms.

The asset management player noted its endeavour remains to continue generating superior risk-adjusted returns for our investors, backed by our dynamic style of money management.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"As we move forward, we will invest further in expanding our teams and capabilities. We shall devote ourselves to further fortification of our infrastructure and investment in the expansion of our team," it added.

The mutual fund has made the following hires over the past nine months:

  • Shashi Kataria, CFO & Director, brings 20 years of expertise from PPFAS AMC.
  • Usha Lakshmi Raman, the new CCO, has over 30 years of experience, previously holding leadership roles at SBI AMC and JPMorgan.
  • Sudha Biju, Head of Investor Services, joins with two decades of experience from HDFC AMC and HSBC Asset Management.
  • Premprakash Dubey, Head of Operations, adds 19 years of expertise from PPFAS AMC and DSP Mutual Fund.
  • Yogesh Kuwad as a consultant for process reviews
  • Sameer Kate as a proposed money manager
  • Lastly, a new CEO with regional and global expertise is expected to join by April 2025.
Also Read | Quant Mutual Fund takes about half of Adani Enterprises' Rs 4,200 crore QIP

Quant Mutual Fund added that its aim is to establish ourselves as a trusted wealth manager for the many, guiding an ever-growing number of Indian households toward a more secure and prosperous financial future. "We extend our most heartfelt gratitude to you for being a most essential part of our endeavours," wrote the fund in its December factsheet.

The restructuring comes as Quant's growing corpus draws regulatory scrutiny, particularly concerning compliance and operational safeguards. Market regulator SEBI recently conducted search-and-seizure operations at Quant Mutual Fund in June, underscoring the need for robust governance. No other action has been taken following the search and seizure. By beefing up its leadership bandwidth, Quant seems to align more closely with regulatory expectations while preparing for its next phase of growth.

Quant Mutual Fund is founded by Sandeep Tandon. The fund got a mutual fund license from the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) in 2017. It has been the fastest growing mutual fund in the country, with assets growing from Rs 100-odd crore in 2019 to more than Rs 1 lah crore currently.

data.title: Torrent Power may raise Rs 3,500 crore, launches QIP for 4.6% equity stake sale; check share price
data.description:
Torrent Power will use the proceeds from the QIP for repaying borrowings, and general corporate purposes.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Torrent Power on Monday opened its Qualified Institutional Placement (QIP), seeking to raise potentially Rs 3,500 crore by selling 4.6 percent of the company’s equity stake. The issue’s indicative price has been set at Rs 1,503 per share, representing a 5.3 percent discount to the stock's last closing price and a 3.4 percent discount to the floor price, reported CNBC TV18.

The floor price for the QIP was set at Rs 1,555.75 per equity share, as announced earlier, representing a 1.6 percent discount to Torrent Power’s closing price of Rs 1,586.3 on NSE on Monday. The company determined the floor price based on SEBI regulations, it said in a stock exchange filing.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The proceeds from the QIP will be used for repaying certain borrowings of Torrent Power and its subsidiaries and for general corporate purposes. The issue includes a 90-day lock-in period for participants.

Also read | Godrej Properties QIP saw four-fold demand, Pirojsha says funds to help in growth

Reports earlier suggested that Torrent Power, the energy arm of the Gujarat-based Torrent Group, planned to raise between Rs 3,000 crore and Rs 4,000 crore through the QIP to fund acquisitions and organic growth initiatives, including capital expenditure for new projects and working capital requirements.

Torrent Power shares jumped 5 percent to end at Rs 1,586.3 on the NSE on Monday, taking the company's market capitalisation to Rs 76,240 crore. The stock has gained 68.37 percent so far this year, significantly outperforming the benchmark Sensex, which rose 11.04 percent during the same period.

The QIP was approved by shareholders earlier this year with a ceiling of Rs 5,000 crore.

In Q2 FY25, Torrent Power's net profit fell 8.6 percent on-year to Rs 496 crore. The revenue from operations gained to Rs 7,175.81 crore, higher by 3.1 percent, compared to Rs 6,960.92 crore in the year-ago period.

Story continues below Advertisement

data.title: KPIT Tech to recover after recent underperformance, says JPMorgan
data.description:
KPIT Technologies Ltd

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

JPMorgan has reiterated its 'Overweight' rating on automobile-industry-focused KPIT Technologies, citing the company's potential for recovery despite recent underperformance.

Over the past year, KPIT Tech's stock has declined by 10 percent, underperforming the Nifty 50's 17 percent gain. The stock has also posted negative monthly returns for five consecutive months. With a target price of Rs 1,900, JPMorgan foresees a potential upside of 42 percent from the last closing price of Rs 1,342.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

JPMorgan believes that the near-term softness in the company's performance is cyclical and transient, not structural. While the current pace of investments in EV and hybrid technologies has slowed, the brokerage said that such investments will continue, though at a measured pace.

Follow our live blog for all the market action

Additionally, the board of KPIT Tech had approved raising up to Rs 2,880 crore through Qualified Institutional Placements (QIP) which JPMorgan said will be used for tuck-in mergers and acquisitions.

A tuck-in acquisition is a business strategy in which a larger company acquires a smaller firm within the same or a related industry and integrates its operations into an existing division or business unit. Typically, the smaller company ceases to exist as an independent entity after the acquisition.

Calling KPIT Tech 'a fallen hero that can rise again,' JPMorgan projects a base-case potential upside of 42 percent, with a bear-case downside capped at 10 percent.

Also Read | JPMorgan names KPIT Tech as top pick on expected auto sector revival in 2025

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The brokerage had recently named KPIT Tech its top pick among ER&D firms, citing expectations of a recovery in the auto sector to drive growth from 2025. "Auto weakness is more cyclical and transient, and growth should accelerate from next year," JPMorgan said.

Financially, KPIT Tech delivered strong results in Q2FY25, with consolidated net profit rising 44 percent year-on-year to Rs 203.7 crore and revenue increasing 22.7 percent to Rs 1,471.4 crore. Sequentially, revenue grew by 7.8 percent, although profits dipped marginally by 0.2 percent. The company has guided FY25 revenue growth at 18-22 percent, with an EBITDA margin expectation of 20.5 percent.

data.title: Godrej Properties QIP saw four-fold demand, Pirojsha says funds to help in growth
data.description:
"This capital raise will allow GPL to significantly enhance its business development portfolio while maintaining a tremendously strong balance sheet," Pirojsha Godrej said.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Realtor Godrej Properties has closed its placement of shares to institutional investors - the largest QIP by a real estate company - after raising Rs 6,000 crore with nearly four-fold higher demand for the issue, the company said on December 3.

Pirojsha Godrej, Executive Chairperson, Godrej Properties said the funds will be utilized for growth capital. "We are delighted with the response to our QIP process. We greatly appreciate the confidence and support of the investment community and will do our best to ensure we utilize this capital wisely," Pirojsha added.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The company saw long only demand from investors, a statement said, "The issue saw a strong demand of ~4X the size of the QIP and was fully covered with long only demand." Godrej confirmed that the had a healthy mix of leading international and domestic investors such as GIC, Blackrock, Aberdeen, Norges Bank, SBI Pension, as well as ICICI Prudential life Insurance.

"This capital raise will allow GPL to significantly enhance its business development portfolio while maintaining a tremendously strong balance sheet," Pirojsha Godrej said.

Read More: HSBC sees no weakness in real estate demand, recommends top Buys

GPL added that the QIP has resulted in the net worth increasing by more than 50%, while the equity dilution is 7.68%. After the issue, GPL’s Net-debt-to-equity ratio will improve to below 0.2:1 from 0.7:1 as of September 30.

Jeffries, Morgan Stanley, Bank of America, and Kotak were the bankers for the QIP.

The realtor has been clocking 'tremendous growth' in its bookings, which has grown from 56% in FY23 to 84% in FY24. For H1FY25, GPL has achieved a bookings growth of 90%, and the company said it is set to 'substantially surpass' its FY24 booking value guidance of Rs 27,000 crore. Godrej Properties was the largest real estate developer in terms of bookings in FY25.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"GPL is well positioned to deliver a 3rd consecutive year of rapid growth in booking value with 90% growth in H1 bookings, launches planned across 7 cities in Q3, and an even stronger
launch pipeline for Q4," Pirojsha Godrej said.
data.author: MoneyControl
